pages.text: 290-21  November 23, 2021  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   Essex   Green   is   the   owner   of   two   parcels   located   West   Orange   Township,  known as Block 155.21; Lot 40   and   Block 155; Lot 40.03.   (the   “ properties ” );  WHEREAS ,   West   Orange   Towns hip   filed   property   tax   appeals   seeking   an   increase   in  assessm ent at the properties for tax years 2016 through 2021;  WHEREAS ,   Essex   Green   filed   property   tax   appeals   seeking   a   decrease   in   the   property  assessments for the 2018 through 2021 tax years;  WHERE AS ,   the   Parties   wish   to   amicably   settle   the   property   tax   appea ls   and   assessments  for all pending tax years and up to and including the   2016 through   2023 tax years;  WHEREAS ,   West   Orange   has   furnished   Essex   Green   with   a   proposed   Library   Signage  plan and esti mated costs ;  WHEREAS ,   the   Parties   settlement   of   the   tax   assess ments   appeals   will   incorporate   this  agreement to include Essex Green ’ s contribution towards Library Signage to be constructed;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   authorizes   the   Mayor   to   e x ecute   the   Ag reement   and   effectuate   the   settlement,   and   the  Township Clerk shall be authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature .  Karen J. Carnevale,   R.M.C.   C in d y Ma tut e- Brown  Municipal Clerk   Counc i l President  Adop ted: N ovember 23, 2021

SETTLEMENT   AGREEMENT  SETTLEMENT   AGREEMENT   (this   “Agreement”),   dated   November   __,   2021,   between  CLPF   Essex   Green,   LLC   (“Essex   Green”);   and   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   New   Jersey (“Township”).   Essex   Green   and   West   Orange   are   sometimes   referred   to   herein   individually   as   a “Party”   and   collectively   as the   “Parties.” WHEREAS,   Essex   Green   is   the   owner   of   two   parcels   located   West   Orange   Township,  known   as   Block   155.21;   Lot   40   and   Block   155;   Lot   40.03.   (the   “properties™);  WHEREAS,   West   Orange   Township   filed   property   tax   appeals   seeking   an   increase   in assessment   at the properties   for tax   years   2016   through   2021; WHEREAS,   Essex   Green   filed   property   tax   appeals   seeking   a   decrease   in   the   property assessments   for the   2018   through   2021   tax   years; WHEREAS,   the   Parties   wish   to   amicably   settle   the   property   tax   appeals   and   assessments for   all pending   tax   years   and   up   to   and   including   the   2016   through   2023   tax   years; WHEREAS,   West   Orange   has   furnished   Essex   Green   with   a   proposed   Library   Signage plan   and   estimated   costs   (To   Be   Attached   as Exhibits), WHEREAS,   the   Parties   settlement   of   the   tax   assessments   appeals   will   incorporate   this  agreement   to   include   Essex   Green’s   contribution   towards   Library   Signage   to   be   constructed; NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   mutual   promises,   covenants,   and   releases  herein   and   for   other   good   and   valuable   consideration,   the   receipt   and   sufficiency   of   which   are acknowledged,   the   Parties   agree   as   follows: 1.   2016   to   2020   Property   Tax   Appeals.   The   Parties   agree   to   execute stipulations   of   settlement   for   all   pending   tax   appeals   for   tax   years   2016   through 2020   affirming   the   assessments   currently   on   the   assessment   rolls   for   each   year.  (Attach   as Exhibit)  2.   2021   Property   Tax   Appeal.   The   Parties   agree   to   execute   a  stipulation   of settlement   affirming   the   current   assessment   for Block   155.21;   Lot   40  at the   current   level   of 70,357,100.   The   Parties   further   agree   to   execute   a stipulation of settlement   for   Block   155;   Lot   40.03   at the   current   level   of   1,391,300.   (Attach   as  Exhibit)  3.   2022   and   2023   Property   Tax   Assessments.   The   Parties   agree   that  the   2022   and   2023   assessments   for   Block   155.21;   Lot   40   shall   be   set   at 60,357,100.  The   Parties   further   agree   that   the   2022   and   2023   assessments   for   Block   155;   Lot 40.03   shall   be   1,391,300.  4.   Essex   Green   Library   Signage   Contribution.   Essex   Green   shall   be responsible   for   the   design,   fabrication   and   installation   of the   library   signage   up   to a   cost   of   $75,000   (“Signage   Expenditure”).   All   Signage   Expenditures   shall   be subject   to pre-approval   by   the   Township   in writing.

s   Library   Signage   Plan. Cost   Estimates,   Design   and   Placement.   Essex Green   shall   work   with   Township   in finalizing   and   implementing   the   installation   of signs   to be   placed   on   the   subject   properties   to   be   within   the   Signage   Budget   which shall be based   off of the Library’s   Wayfinding   Survey   by Arcari   + Iovino   Architects P.C.   annexed   hereto   as Exhibit   “A”.  6.   West   Orange   Library   Signage   Contribution.   Should   the   Library Signage   exceed   the   Signage   Budget,   Township   shall   be   responsible   for   the remaining   costs;   provided   that   any   additional   costs   must   be   preapproved   in writing by   the   Township   Chief Financial   Officer.  7.   No   Admission.   The   Parties   acknowledge   and   agree   that the purpose of this   Agreement   is   to   finally   and   fully   resolve   all   disputes   between   the   Parties regarding   any   assessments   and   with   respect   to   the   design,   fabrication,   and placement   of the   Library   Signage. 8.   Governing   Law.   This   Agreement   will be   governed   and   construed   in accordance   with   the laws   of the   State   of New   Jersey,   without   regard   for its conflicts of laws   principles.  9.   Amendments.   No   waiver   or modification   of this Agreement,   or any portion   hereof,   will   be   binding   unless   in writing   and   signed   by   both   Parties. 10.   Construction.   No   Party   will   be   deemed   the   drafter   of   this  Agreement.  11.   Entire   Agreement.   This   Agreement   constitutes   the   entire   agreement between   the   Parties   with   respect   to   subject   matter   of   this   Agreement,   and supersedes   all   prior   and   contemporaneous   oral   and   written   agreements   and discussions   pertaining   to the   subject   matter   of this   Agreement.  12.   Successors   and   Assigns.   This   Agreement   is binding   upon   and   inures to the   benefit   of the   Parties   and   their   respective   administrators,   agents,   employees,  representatives,   partners,   parents,   subsidiaries,   divisions,   affiliates,   lessors, officers,   related   entities,   invitees,   licensees,   directors,   shareholders,   investors, attorneys,   transferors,   transferees,   predecessors,   successors,   trustees   in bankruptcy,  assigns,   and   elected   and   appointed   officials.  13.   Authority.   Each   Party represents   and   warrants   to the other Party that it has   all necessary   right,   title,   and   authority   to   enter   into   this   Agreement,   to   grant the   rights   and   interests   herein   granted,   and   to   perform   all   of its   obligations   under this   Agreement.   Each   signatory   to   this   Agreement   represents   and   warrants   that   it has   the   authority   to execute   this Agreement   and   that such   signature   is binding   upon such   signatory’s   Party. 14.   Counterparts.   This   Agreement   may   be   executed   in counterparts   and when   each   Party   has   signed   and   delivered   at least   one   such   counterpart   to the   other Party,   each   counterpart   will   be   deemed   an   original,   and   all   counterparts   taken together   will   constitute   one   and   the   same   agreement,   which   will   be   binding   and  2

effective   as to all Parties.   This   Settlement   Agreement   may   be executed   via facsimile or pdf signatures,   which   will   have   the   same   force   and   effect   as if they   were   original signatures,   to be   followed   by   executed   originals. [Signature   Page   Follows]

IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   the   Parties   have   caused   this   Agreement   to   be   signed   by   their  duly   authorized   representatives. 291-21  Nove mber 23, 2021  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE COLLECTOR OF TAXES   TO REBATE PAYMENT TO THE  LISTED TAXPAYER   PURSUANT TO FINAL JUDGMENT BY THE TAX COURT   OF NEW JERSEY  WHEREAS, final judgment on tax appeals has been rendered by the Tax Court of New Jersey  on the petitions of the taxpayers listed on the attached schedule reducing the assessment.  WHEREAS, such judgment by merit of N.J.S.A. 54:3-27.2 shall be con clusive and binding upon the  municipal assessor and taxing district;  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the West Orange Township Council that the Collector  of Taxes and Treasurer of West Orange, be and they are hereby authorized, empowered and directed   to  cause to be paid to the said taxpayers the sums in full and final satisfaction to tax rebates due:

documents.title: BENECARD RENEWAL RESOLUTION

pages.text: 294-21  November 23, 2021  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS, on or about September 17, 2013, the Township of West Orange Council  authorized the Township of West Orange (the   “ Township ” ) to enter into a Contract for Employee  Prescription Drug Insurance (the   “ Agreement ” )   with Benecard Services, Inc. ( “ Benecard ” ); and  WHEREAS, the Township seeks to renew the Agreement with Benecard for a   one -year  term (the   “ Term ” ) from January 1, 2022 through December 31, 2022 (the   “ Amendment ” ); and  WHEREAS, the monthly guaranteed rates f or the Term are set forth in the Amendm ent,  annexed hereto as Attachment   “ A; ”  WHEREAS, the Mayor has recused himself from the renewal of the Agreement,  including the execution of any documents related to the renewal of the Agreement.  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RE SOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE  T OWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, that the Township shall and hereby does authorize the  Amendment, at the guaranteed rates set forth therein; and be it further  RESOLVED, that the Chief Financial Officer be and is hereby author ized to execute all  documents necessary   to effectuate the Amendment; and be it further  RESOLVED that this Resolution shall be published and made available in the Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. C arnevale, R.M.C ., Municipal C lerk   Cindy Matute-Brown , Council President  Adopted: November 23, 2021

29 5-21  November 23, 2021  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING AN AGREEMENT WITH BERKLEY ACCIDENT  AND HEALTH FOR STOP LOSS COVERAGE FOR 2022  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange ’ s Insurance Broker solicited offers and proposals for  Stop Loss Coverage for the Township ’ s 2021 Health Insurance Coverage; and  WHEREAS, the Township ’ s Chief Financial Officer recommends the proposal submitted by Sun  L ife, annexed hereto; and  WHEREAS, the Agreement with Sun Life will save West Orange Taxpayers BETWEEN $80,000  AND $100,000, over the incumbent provider ’ s proposal; and  WHEREAS, the Mayor has recused himself from the award of this Agreement, including the  execution of any documents related to this Agreement.  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP  OF WEST ORANGE, that the Township shall and hereby does authorize the Agreement, at the  guaranteed rates set forth therein; and  BE I T FURTHER RESOLVED, that the Chief Financial Officer be and is hereby authorized to  execute all documents necessary to effectuate the Agreement; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that this Resolution shall be published and made available in the  Clerk ’ s Office fo r reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carne vale, R.M. C., Munici pal Clerk   Cindy Mat ute-Brown , Cou ncil President  Adopted: November 23, 2021

297-21  November 23, 2021  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange received a grant from the New Jersey Department of  Transportation (NJDOT) for MA   –   2020 Arverne Road, Crestmont Road (portion) and Westover Terrace  (portion) Improvement Project in the amount of $625,000.00; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange received a grant from the NJDOT for MA   –   2021  Various   Roads, Improvement Project, Crestmont Road (remainder), Lenox Terrace and Masson Place in  the amount of $505,000.00; and  WHEREAS , due to the Covid   –   19 pandemic, the design of the MA   –   2020 project was delayed  until 2021; and  WHEREAS , to achieve econ omies of scale the Township Engineer requested a proposal for  design services that would combine both the MA   –   2020 project and the MA   –   2021 project into one bid  package; and  WHEREAS ,   by Resolution No.   164 - 21 dated July 13, 2021 a professional servic es contract was  awarded   to the engineering firm of Greenman   –   Pedersen   (GPI)   of Morris Plains, NJ in the amount of  $58,500.00 to develop plans, specifications and bid documents for a combined MA   –   2020 and MA   –  2021 project; and  WHEREAS , the NJDOT Loca l Aid office in Newark, NJ would allow the combining of both  grant year projects provided the MA   –   2020 project was awarded by November 21, 2021; and  WHEREAS , the cost of the improvements of the combined project exceeded $1,000,000.00  which would requi re an environmental review by the NJDOT   known as EO-215 ; and  WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer, expressed concerns the environmental review of the  project which proposed curb in locations where no curb existed would require storm water management  improve ments which weren ’ t in the project ’ s budget; and

WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer directed Greenman   –   Pedersen to separate the MA   –   2020  project from the combined package to lower the project cost; and  WHEREAS , the separation of the projects delayed t he completion of the design for the MA   –  2020 project; and  WHEREAS , GPI completed the   redesign for the MA   –   2020 project in September   and   submit ted  them for   review by the NJDOT Local Aid Office in Newark   on September 7, 2021 ; and  WHEREAS , the MA   –   2020 project cost was estimated by the design engineer to be over  $1,000,000.00 which was higher than the Municipal Engineer ’ s estimate when the MA   –   2020 grant  application was submitted; and  WHEREAS , the MA   –   2020 project still required an   EO-215   environmental review   as advised  by the NJDOT on September 10, 2021 ; and  WHEREAS , this review added further delay to the project schedule, and  WHEREAS , the Local Aid Office issued design comments on September 14, 2021   and received  on September 2 0, 2021 with a revised comment letter received on September 27, 2021 ; and  WHEREAS , GPI needed time to address those comments; and  WHEREAS , the   EO-215   environmental clearance for the project was received on September 29,  2021; and  WHEREAS , GPI submitt ed plans for review on October 21, 2021 which were approved by the  Local Aid Office on October 22, 2021; and  WHEREAS , the bid and award of the MA   –   2020 project could still meet the award deadline of  November 21, 2021 if a short bid period was specifie d; and  WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer to promote more competition and competitive prices  decided a longer bid period beyond the November   21 , 2021   award   deadline was beneficial; and

WHEREAS , the Township advertised for bids on November 1, 2021 with a bid receipt date of  November 30, 2021; and  WHEREAS , the bid receipt date allows for sufficient time for the Municipal Engineer to  recommend an award for the December 14, 2021 Township Council   meeting; and  WHEREAS ,   due to   the adoption on second reading of the Finance Ordinance for 2021  Capital Projects on October 25, 2021 ,   sufficient funds are available to award a contract for the MA   –   2020  project on December 14, 2021; and  WHEREAS , an award on December 14, 2021 requires the NJDOT to grant an extension of time  for the contract award; and  WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer recommends a contract extension to December 20, 2021 or  30 days be g ranted; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange, Essex County, New Jersey   that the Township of West Orange   hereby requests an additional 30-  day exte nsion of time to award a construction contract for the MA   –   2020 NJDOT Municipal Aid Project  to   December 20, 2021.  Karen J. Carnevale, RMC   Cindy Matute   –   Brown  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: November 23, 2021

2 98 -21  November 23 , 2021  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; a n d  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time   w h e n   t h e   minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A.  10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discuss i o n s ;   n ow , therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   November 23, 2021   enter into a closed session to consider the  following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an   i nvasion of individual  privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized t o   p ro t e ct   t h e sa fety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of   W e st   O ran g e   Adm inistrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentia l i t y   i s  r equi red in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ( )   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be r e n d e r ed   co n f iden tial.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisi t i o n   of   re a l   pro perty  with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a publ i c   h e ar ing   t hat   may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  T he   E nvironmental   A nalysis of the Prism Properties  ( the   general subject matter ( s ) ) of discussion shall b e   indi cated by   a check mark in the appro priat e box   an d  s ha ll be   more   speci fic ally   re ferred t o thereafter   to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality   giving rise to the closed   session.  IT IS FURTH E R   RES OLVED   tha t   the minutes of said dis cussi on sha ll   b e   m ade pu blic   as  so on   as t he   matter u nder discussi on is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be s erved by such confidentia lity.  The foregoing   r e so luti on was du ly adopted by the Townshi p Cou ncil o f   th e   T ownshi p of   West   Ora nge   at   a  public   meeting held   on   November 23, 2021.  Motion :  Second :  Vote  Aye :  Opposed :  Karen J. Carnevale , R .M.C.   Cindy Matute-Brown  M unicipal Clerk   Counci l Pres ident  A d opted:   November 23 ,   2021

pages.text: 304-21  November 23, 2021  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   May   3,   2016,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   adopted  Resolution   121-16   appointing   Matrix   New   World   ( “ Matrix ” ),   with   offices   at   26   Columbia  Turnpike,   Florham   Park,   New   Jersey   as   the   Licensed   Site   Remediation   Professional   (the  “ LSRP ” )   to   assist   and   oversee   the   investigation   and   cleanup   of   certain   properties   on   the   Central  Avenue   (the   “ Redevelopment   Area ” )   as   to   the   suspected   discharge   of   hazardous   substances   or  hazardous waste; and  WHEREAS,   on   October   26,   2016,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )  adopted   Resolution   228-16   designating   certain   properties   on   Central   Avenue   (the  “ Redevelopment   Area ” )   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment ,   including   the   properties   located   at  18   Central   Avenue,   Block   9,   Lot   36   and   4   Tompkins   Street,   Block   7,   Lot   22   on   the   Tax   Map   of  the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Selecto-Flash   Property ” )   and   the   property   at   8-10   Central  Avenue,   Block   9,   Lot   3 2   on   the   Township   Tax   Map   also   known   as   and   referred   to   as   4-10  Central Avenue (the   “ Biddleman Property ” ) (collectively the   “ Properties ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   acquired   the   Selecto-Flash   Property   through   an   In   Rem   Tax  Foreclosure and on   October 2, 2018, the Township acquired title to the   Biddleman   Property ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   r emains   committed   to   the   remediation   and   redevelopment   of  the Redevelopment Area including the Properties ; and  WHEREAS,   the   remediation   of   the   Properties   has   reach ed   the   stage   where   R emedial  A ction   is needed ; and  WHEREAS,   Matrix   has   prepared   a   scope   of   work   outlining   the   proposed   Remedial  Action   for   the   Properties   and   an   application   to   New   Jersey   Department   of   Environment  Protection   seeking   grant   funds   from   the   Hazardous   Discharge   Site   Remediation   Fund   (the

2  4832-8410-1329, v. 1  “ HDSRF ” )   Program   for   the   purpose   of   funding   the   proposed   Remedial   Action   on   the   Property,  Exhibit   “ A ”   annexed here ; and  WHEREAS,   the   application   for   HDSRF   grant   funding   seeks   the   award   of   $4,675,000   to  fund the Remedial Action on the Property; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   se eks   to   Authorize   Matrix   to   submit   the   application   for  HDSRF Grant funds with the proposed scope of Remedial Action.  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Township   hereby   A uthorize s   Matrix   under   this  Resolution   304-21   to   submit   the   application   for   HDSRF   grant   funds   to   fund   the   implementation  of the Remedial   Action set forth in the proposed scope of work; and   be it further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Cl erk's   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law .  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M .C.   Cindy Matute-Brown  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 2 3,   2021

G000006148  MATRIX NO.   18 - 286 - 01  Dear   M s .   Stopper :  On behalf of the   Township of West Orange ,   Matrix New World Engineering, Land Surveying and  Landscape Architecture, P.C. (Matrix) , is submitting   a n   A pplication for a   Hazardous   Discharge Site  Remediation Fund ( HDSRF )   Grant   to complete the Remedial   Action   ( RA ) at the   former Selecto Flash Site  located at 18 Central Avenue in West Orange, New Jersey   and at the former Biddelman Inc. Site located at  4 - 10 Central Avenue in West Orange,   New Jersey   (Site) .   In his email dated   April 6, 2021 , former NJDEP  Brownfield Coordinator, John Doyon,   requested that   one HDSRF Grant   Application   be submitted for the  proposed remedial actions at the   Selecto Flash and the former Biddelman   properties   as   the properties are  adjoining and the work will   occur   simultaneously.   Combining the remedial actions creates efficiencies and  will save money .  BACKGROUND  Selecto Flash, Inc.  In June 2018 an application for a HDSRF Grant to complete the   RI for the Selecto Flash Site was submitted  to NJDEP. The total of this HDSRF RI Grant was $129,176.80.   While implementing the RI Scope of Work  (SOW) in 2019, a number of monitoring wells were found to be damaged, requiring replacement. At the  suggestion o f NJDEP OBR, Matrix submitted a request on September 30, 2019, for the reallocation of a  portion of the original RI HDSRF funding to allow for the immediate rehabilitation of   the   monitoring wells.  The cost for the rehabilitation of the monitoring wells is   estimated at $20,854. The tasks that were removed

F: \ 2018 \ 18 - 286 Biddleman   -   8 - 10 Central Avenue \ HDSRF \ HDSRF Application RA _Selecto and Biddelman \ HDSRF Cover  Letter.docx  from the original RI SOW to allow for the well rehabilitation   include d   the completion of the RIR and  Remedial Action Workplan (RAWP) . Therefore, an a pplication for a   Supplemental HDSRF Grant   was  submitt ed t o the NJDEP in October 2019 . This application   also include d   costs for   one   additional well  repairs,   two   well abandonments,   one   additional   day of soil sampling,   vapor intrusion sampling,   and one  additional   round of groundwater sampling .   The total   of this HDSRF Supplemental RI Grant was  $44, 718.00.   The RIR/RAWP and associated forms were submitted to the NJDEP in February 2020.  Former Biddelman, Inc.  The Township of West Orange received funding under the NJDEP’s HDSRF Grant Program to complete a  Pr eliminary Assessment (PA) and Site Investigation (SI).   The total of the HDSRF Grant was $63,956.20.  While completing the scope of work, 17 potential Areas of Concern (AOCs) were identified and seven  AOCs were investigation between October 2019 and March 20 20. The PA/SI Report and associated forms  were submitted to the NJDEP in April 2020. In August 2020, an application for a HDSRF Grant to complete  the RI for the former Biddelman Site was submitted to NJDEP. The total of this HDSRF RI Grant was  $198,425.50.   while completing this scope of work, one additional AOC was identified and soil and  groundwater impacts beneath the Site were delineated. The RIR/RAWP and associated forms were  submitted to the NJDEP in September 2021.  Matrix is now submitting an applic ation for a HDSRF Grant to complete the RA for   both   the Selecto Flash  Site   and the Former Biddelman Site . The RA activities   for both Sites   will includ e the removal of   impacted  soils in several AOCs with post remedial groundwater monitoring and the use of e ngineering (i.e. capping),  and institutional controls (i.e. Deed Notice and Classification Exception Area [CEA]) for residual   VOC  impacts and historic fill material.   This application also includes the costs for well repairs ,   well  abandonments ,   well installations,   and additional vapor intrusion sampling and vapor mitigation   on and off -  Site , if required . The total requested in the application   is   $ 4 , 6 75 ,000.00 .   whic h includes the remedial action  at both Selecto Flash and the former Biddelman proper ties.  In accordance with Part VI of the HDSRF Application Checklist, one original and one copy of the complete  HDSRF application are attached, which include:  •   A   detailed   scope of work (Part I Section H) ;  •   An   itemized cost estimate ( Part I   Section I) ;  •   Executed Part II   -   General Certification;  •   Executed Part III   -   Subrogation of Rights;  •   Executed Part V   -   Municipal Questionnaire;   and  •   Township Resolution and Foreclosure documentation.  We thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. If you have   any questions or require any additional  information, do not hesitate to contact us at (973) 240 - 1800.  Sincerely,  Allison Molnar   Richard Britton , P. G000006148  West Orange, Essex County ,   New Jersey  September 2021  1.0   IN TRODUCTION  The foll owing   Remedial Action   S cop e of   W ork   (SO W)   has been prepared   on behalf of   the   Township of  West Orange   (Township)   as part of the request for a   Brownfield Development Area (BDA) grant   from the  New J er se y Depa rtment of Environmental Protection ’s   (NJDEP)   Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation  F und (HDSRF) .   Funding   is being requested   for the   compl etion   of   Remedial   Action   ( R A )   and   the  preparation of   a   Remedial Action   Report   (RAR)   and Response Action O utcom e   (RAO)   for   t he   T ow nship -  owned propert y ,   Former   Selecto Flash, I nc.   ( Site ) l ocated at   18 Cent ral Av enue   and for the T ownship -  owned property , Former Biddelman, Inc.   (Site) located at   4 - 10 Central A venue   in   West Orange , Essex  Co unty, New   Jersey .   The Selecto   F l a sh Site is   approximately   1. 33 - acre s and   the   Former Biddelman Site  is approximately 0.51 - arces   and are   part of the   Central Valley ( Orange West )   BDA . The Sites are   located  in a mixed industrial , comm ercial, and residential area of   the Township .   T he   Centra l   Valley BDA i s  comprise d of six lots (Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32 and 36, and Block 7, Lot 22).   The Site are  Lots 32 and 36 of B lock 9.  The S i te s   are   also included   within the Orange Valley Regional Groundwater C o ntamination   Site  ( Comprehensive   E nvironmental   Response,   Compensation   and   Liability   Act   [CERCLA]   I D   No.  NJD002148799 [form erly   known   as the West Orange Groundwater C o ntamination   Site]), which consists  of a groundwater contamina nt plume that cannot be clearly   attributed   to   any   specific so ur ce. The Site s   are  two   of   22 facilit ies associated with F - listed Wa stes that may be the source, or one of the sources , of the  groundwater contamination.  Selecto Flash Site  T he   Townshi p   acquired   the prope rt y   at   18 Central A venue   (Block   9; Lot 3 6)   in   West   Orange , N ew   J ersey  thr ough foreclosure   in   April   2016 .   The   S ite is identified   by the NJDEP as PI No. 027054   ( Selecto Flash ,  Inc. )   with   Case Tracking No. 95 - 10 - 18 - 1521 - 31.   The former buildings at   the Site   we re   demolished in   the  fall of 2017.   Beginning in the   1 980 ’ s, Selecto Flash   owned and operated a sil k - screen ing busin es s, using  solvent - based inks to pr oduce la rge - s cale decals.   Historical   soil d ata has in dicated the presence of  trichloroethene (TCE) and other chlorinated volatile or ganic contaminants (CVOCs ) at the   Site. TCE has  also   been consistently detect ed in grou nd wa ter samples   collected from on - site monitoring wells.  T he   Townsh i p   received   funding under the NJDEP ’s   HDSRF grant program to complete a Preliminar y  Assessment (PA) a nd   S i te   Investigation   (S I)   at   the Site   to evaluat e the   environmental   condition s.   The PA  R eport   was submitted to   the NJDEP on February 23, 2018   an d identified 22 areas of concern (AOC) .   As  documented   within the   SI   Report   t wo   addition al AOCs were added for a   t otal of 24 AOCs a nd wa s  su bmitted to the NJDEP   on Ma rch 2, 2018.   Environmenta l   investi gations complete d at the Site   during   a  review of historical investigations , the SI, and   Remedial Investigation   ( RI )   activities   ( original   and  supplemental   RI HDSRF   Grant )   have   identified   the pr esen ce of   contaminants, including   CVOCs , semi -

Furthermore,   CVOCs   and SVOCs  were detected in   groundwater   at concent rations above the   NJDEP ’ s   Groundwater Quality Standards  (GWQS)   and   the NJDEP ’ s V apor Intrusion Groundwater Screening L ev els (VIGWSL) .  As p art of the RI   activities conducted on - Site between January 2019 and   December   2019 ,   horizontal   and  vertical   delineation of CVOC ex ceedanc es of RDCSRS and NRDCSRS   was achieved   in all directions.   In  addition, soil texture analysis was co mp let ed for use in the   Seasonal Soil Compartment Model (SESOIL)  Model to develop a Site - Specific I mpa ct to   Groundwater Soil Remediation S tandard (SSIGWSRS) fo r   cis  1,2 - D CE, PCE, and TCE.   The RI for   groundwater (AOC - 16) included the esta blished of a Classif ic ation  Exception   Area   (CEA) for the Selecto Flash portion of the contaminant plume.   Finally,   b ased on the  results of the 2019 groundwater sampling, Vapor Int rusion (VI)   sampling   was   required at   three   occupied  structures   within   100   feet   of   CVOC   contaminati on   above   NJDEP   Vapor   I ntrusion   Groundwater  Scr eening Levels (GWSL)   in three monitoring wells   MW - 1R, MW - 2, and MW - 3R .   These three occu pied  structures were   samp led,   and a completed VI pathway does   not exist .   The RI   Report   and   Remedial Action  Workplan   (RAW)   w ere   submitted to the N JDEP   on   Februa ry   28,   2020.  Former Biddelman, Inc.  The T ownship acquired the property at 10 Central Avenue ( Block 9, Lot 32) in West Orange , New Jersey  in   October 2018. The Site is identified   by the NJDEP as PI No. G000006 148 (Former Biddelman, Inc)  with   Case Tracking N o.   17 - 04 - 20 - 1128 - 26.   From the late 1950’s to 1985 when operations ceased,  Biddelman, Inc. engaged in the wholesale distribution of dry good s and dry - cleaning supplies. The  materials and chemicals involved in this business ranged from orga ni c solvents such as ammonia and  acetic acid for spot cleaning to PCE for dry - cleaning. The PCE was stored in a 4,000 - gallon AST (AOC -  1). The PCE AST was rem oved with a fuel oil UST in 1987. From approximately 1985 to 2004, the Site  was owned by Richard Pr ai tano, who operated a portion of the building as Personalized Letter Service  and Sales CO., Inc., a letter printing business. A portion of the warehouse was   sublet to Artie Petussian of  Tri - State Technical Sales, which assembled plastic forms. All operati on s ceased in April 2004 and the  Site has been vacant since 2004. The former operations associated with the dry - cleaning business are  most likely the source   of the contamination in soils and have contributed to groundwater contamination  beneath the Site.  T h e   Townsh i p   received   funding under the NJDEP ’s   HDSRF grant program to complete a   PA   and S I   at  the Site   to evaluat e the   environmental   condition s.   The PA /SI   R eport   was submitted to   the NJDEP   in April  2020   an d ident if ied   17   AOC .   As   documented   within the   R I   Rep ort   and RAW,   one   addition al AOC   w as  added for a total of   18   AOCs a nd was su bmitted to the NJDEP   on   September 24, 2 021 .   Environmenta l  investi gations complete d at the Site   during   a review of historical investigations , the   SI , and   RI   activities  have   identifie d   the   presen ce   of   contaminants,   includin g   CVOCs ,   SVOCs ,   and   met als   in   soil   at  concentrat ions   exceeding   the   NJDEP   Residential   Inhalation   Exposure   Pathway   Soil   Remediation  Standards   (RISRS),   Residential   Ingestion - Dermal   Ex po sure   Pathway   Soil   Remediation   Stan dards  (RIDSRS),   and/or   Migration   to   Groundwater   Exposure   Pathway   Soil   Remediation   Standards  (MGWSRS) .   Furthermore,   CVOCs,   and SVOCs were detected in   groundwater   at concent rations above  the   NJDEP ’ s   GWQS   and   NJDEP   VIGWSL .  As part of the RI   activities condu cted on - Site between   March 202 1 and J une 2021 ,   horizontal delineation  was   achieved   of CVOC exceedances of the NJDEP RISRS in soils surrounding the AOCs .   Additionally,  ver tical delineation was also achieved of the impacte d   soils at the Site at a several dep ths ranging from  6.5 ft bgs to 12.5 ft bgs .   A SSMGWSRS was developed for PCE for on - site and off - site impacts using the

Based on the SSMGWSRS developed for TCE and vinyl chloride, Site   soils have been  delineated horizontally and vertical ly .   The RI for   groundwater (AOC - 16) included the   delineat ion of th e  gr oundwater impacts   and   esta blish ment   of a   CEA   for the   Bidd el man   portion of the contaminant plume.  Finally,   b ased on the results of the   202 0   groundwater sampling,   VI   sampling   was   required   at   five  occupied structures within 100 feet of CVOC contamination   above NJDEP   GWSL   in   o n - site   monitoring  wells .   These   structures were   samp led,   and a completed VI pathway does   not exist .   The RI   Report   and  RAW were   submitted to the NJDEP   on   S eptember 24, 2021 .  The   RA   pla nned   for both Sites   in clud e   the removal of   impacted   soils in several AOCs with post remedial  groundwater monitori ng   and the use of engineering (i.e . capping), and   institutional   controls (i.e. Deed  Notice and CEA) for residual   VOC   impac ts and   historic   fill material .   After the complet ion of the RA and  redeve lopment of the Site, a Deed Notice will be prepared and fil ed wit h the County Clerk of Essex  County.   A copy of the filed Deed Notice will be included with an   a pplication for   a Remedial Action  Permit for Soils ( RAP - s oils ) as part of the R A   Report . Should co ntamination remain in groundwater   af ter  post - remedial monitoring is comple ted, the groundwater CEA w ill be re - evaluated and   modified as  necessary, with   detail   included   in   an application for a Remedial Action   Permi t   for Groundwater ( RAP -  Groundwater ). Upon   a pproval   of t he RAPs, a Restricted   Use   Response Action Outcome ( RAO ) for the  Site will   be   issued by the Licensed Site Remediation   Pro fessional (LSRP).  2.0   TECHNICAL APPROACH  Th is SOW   has been developed in compliance   with   the   requirements of the   Sit e Remediation Ref orm Act  (SRRA),   N.J.S.A   58:10C - 1   et   seq.,   the   Administrative   Re quirements   for   the   Remediation   of  Contaminated   Site s (ARRCS),   N.J.A.C. 7 :26C,   t he   Technical   Requirements for   Si te   Remediation  (TRSR), N.J.A.C. 7:26E ,   and applicable NJDEP Guida nce Documents. Ov ersight of investigation  activities will be conducted by a Matr ix   LSR P .  The proposed   RA   will include excavation and off - site disposal of   all   i mpacted soils with TCE, PCE ,   and  vinyl chloride that e xceed the   NJDEP ’ s NRDCSRS, RDCSRS, and SS IGWSRS   (Selecto Flash   S i te)   and  NJDEP ’ s RISRS, NRISRS, RIDSRS, NRIDSRS, and SSMGWSRS   (Former Bidd elman   Site)   with in the  unsaturated   zone.   Prior to initiating the R A   activities, New Je rs ey One Call will be notified , all permits  will be   obtained, and a Soil   E rosion and   Sediment Control (SESC) P an will be   completed   and submitted  to the Essex County Soil and Water C o nservation   District (SWCD) in   accordance with N.J.A .C. 7:26E -  5.6 .   All app r ov ed SE SC measures   will be installed   prior   to the start of the   RA   activit ies. F: \ 2018 \ 18 - 286 Biddleman   -   8 - 10 Central Avenue \ HDSRF \ HDSRF Application RA _Selecto and Biddelman \ HDSRF Selecto Flash_Biddelman  RA SOW.doc   Pag e   6   of   8  Following   post - excavation soil sampling activities and before   select ed   hot spot   excavations   are backfilled,  an amendment (ABC+) will be   introduced   into   the   excavation to   accelerate the   reduction   groundwater  concentrations   above NJDEP Class II - A GWQS.   Redox Tech’s ABC+ is an emulsified vegetable oil and  fatty acid ester product for anaerobic bioremediation. ABC+ also contains a phosphate   buffer to maintain  the pH in the optimal range for complete biodegradation.   Subsequent to   ABC+   introduc tion   into the  excavation, zero valent iron (ZVI) will be   blended   into the bottom of   the excavation.   ZVI will abiotically  reduce chlorinated compounds and   accelerate the decrease of dissolved oxygen and oxidation reduction  potential fav orable for in situ biodegradation.   The excavations will then be backfilled as discussed above.  A Permit by Rule (PBR) Au thorization Request will be applied for with the NJDEP in order to   add the  above amendments to the bottom of the   selected hot spot   excavations.  For residual VOC impacts (VOC impacts below residential soil remediation standards but above  MGWSRS),   engineering controls (i.e., capping) will be required to preclude infiltration of   precip it ation as  required by NJDEP Capping of Volatile Contaminants for the Impact to Ground Water Pathway (January  2019, Version 1.1). Engineering controls will consist of   future asphalt and/or concrete paving, and  building coverage related to the planne d futur e   Site development which will provide protection from  direct contact with contaminated soils and prevent leachate forming that could impact groundwater  quality. Curren t redevelopment plans for the Site, consists mostly of an asphalt paved parking lo t;  howe ve r, at the east corner of the Biddelman Site limited grass/permeable areas are planned. All soil  above site specific MGWSRS values will be excavated from this area in   order to preclude any future  impact to groundwater quality. A.C. 7:26E) as w ell as the Solid Waste  Regu lations ( N.J.A.C. 7:26   2A) and the NJDEP ’ s V a por   Intrusion   Technical   G uidance (January 2018,  Version 4.1). Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36  and Block 7, Lot 22 -   Redevelopment Plan  1  1.   REDEVELOPMENT PLAN BACKGROUND  1.1   INTRODUCTION  This Redevelopment Area,   hereinafter   referred to as the “ Central Avenue Redevelopment Area , ” comprises   s ix  lots ( collectively the “ Subject Parcels ” ),   located in the   Township   of   West Orange   ( the “ Township ” ) , Essex  County, New Jersey, as shown in   Figure   2 , USGS Topographic Map   and   Figure   3 , Aerial Photograph of  Project Site .   The subject parcels are located on the following block   and lot s:  A.   Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36  B.   Block 7, Lot 22  The   Central Avenue Redevelopment Area   is   situated   adjacent to the Harvard Press Redevelopment   Area to its  east.   Harvard Press was designated as an Area in Need of Redevelopment   due to being   an abandoned industrial  area subjected to a Brownfields remediation program due to environmental contamination. Further more , both a  portion of the   Central Avenue Redevelopment   Area   (Block 9, Lots 32 and 36) and the Harvard Press  Redevelopment Area are part   of the Central Valley Brownfield Development Area ( the “ CV BDA ” )   under   the  Office of Brownfield Reuse Brownfield Development Area Program. The   CVBDA   was   established   on December  5, 2005 .  The   Township   recognizes that the   Central Avenue Redevelopment Area   parcels   suffe r   from   one or more of the  following conditions :   substandard and dilapidated conditions, vacancy for at least 10 years, environmental  contamination, use in   conflict   with modern land use standards and practice, and a lack of proper utilization.   The  Township   s upports its redevelopment pursuant to the criteria referenced in this Redevelopment Plan.  T his document is   intended   to provide a coordinated Redevelopment Plan for the   Central Avenue Redevelopment  Area   that will facilitate the redevelopment of the Subject   Parcels   in a manner consistent with   Township   objectives  while ma ximizing   development   flexibility given   the existing   environmental constraints .  On   October 5 ,   2016,   the Township   of West Orange   Planning Board   (the “Planning Board”)   adopted Resolution  Number   16 - 03 ,   Appendix A,   which set forth a recommendation to the Township Council that the Subject Parcels  be designated as a Condemnation Area in Need of Redevelopment. The recommendations are bas ed upon the  findings outlined in the “Area in Need of Redevelopment Study” dated July 11, 2016, prepared by the authors of  this Plan, on behalf of the Township Planning Board, to determine whether the   Subject   Parcels meet the statutory  criteria   to   be   desig nated   as   an   Area   in   Need   of   Redevelopment.   Based   upon   the   Planning   Board’s  recommendation, on   October   26 , 2016, the Township Council adopted Resolution Number   228 - 16 ,   Appendix B ,  which designated the Subject Parcels as a Condemnation   Area in Need of Redeve lopment.  The   Tow nship   Council authorized the preparation of this Redevelopment Plan through the adoption of Resolution  N umber, 12 0 - 16   on   May 3 , 2016 ,   Appendix   C . P ursuant to the Local Redevelopment and Housing   Law, a  redevelopment plan may b e adopted ( N.J.S.A.   40A:12A - 7) and implemented ( N.J.S.A.   40A:12A - 15). Th is  Redevelopment   Plan   specifically replaces   and   supersedes   any and   all   previous   zoning   that   govern s   this  Redevelopment Area.  1.2   REDEVELOPMENT   AREA   BOUNDARIES  The   Central Avenue Redevelopment   Area   is comprised of six parcels   having an area of approximately 2.5 acres ,  identified   on the Township Tax Maps as shown in   Figure s   1A & 1B , Tax Map .   Each lot is identified below,  including its street address and property owner,   as   follows:

Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36  and Block 7, Lot 22 -   Redevelopment Plan  2  A.   Block 9, Lot   11.01   –   25 Mitchell Street, German & Graciela Montesdeoca  B.   Block 9 ,   Lot   11.02   –   29 Mitchell Street, Anthony Dennis  C.   Block 9, Lot   11.03   –   27 Mitchell Street, James Dezao  D.   Block 9 ,   Lot   32   –   8 - 10 Central Avenue,   8 Central Ave Properties, LLC   (the   “ Biddleman   S ite ” )  E.   Block 9 ,   Lot   36   –   18 Central Avenue, Selecto - Flash , Inc.   (the “Selecto - Flash Site”) . *  F.   Block 7, Lot 22   –   4 Tompkins Street, Selecto - Flash ,   Inc.   (the “Selecto - Flash Lot”). *  *   Township of West Orange (owner pending)  The parcels   listed above   include a   range   of   residential, commercial, industrial, and   vacant   propert ies  The topography of the   Redevelopment Area   is relatively flat, as   confirmed   on the USGS Topographic Map   as  shown in   Figure   2 ,   USGS Topographic M ap .   The   Redevelopment Area is   delineated   in   Figure   3 ,   Aerial  Photograph of Project Site .  The   Subject Parcels are   located in   the   I Industry District and the R - M Multi - family Residences District as shown  on   Figure 4, Zoning Map .   Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32 and 36 are located within the I In dustry District  and Block 7, Lot 22 is located within the R - M Multi - family Residences District.   R esidential uses   are not a  permitted use   in the I Industry District ,   under Chapter   25   of the   West Orange   Zoning Regulations ; however ,   three  of the lots located   in the I Industry District , Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, and 11.03,   are developed with   residential  uses as well as the southwestern portion of Block 9 along Mitchell Street.   Further more ,   the R - M Multi - family  Residences District includ es   the vacant site of   B lock 7, Lot 22 , which is   within the   Redevelopment Area . To the  west of the   Redevelopment Area   is the B - 1 Retail Business District.   T his Redevelopment Plan establishes a   new  zoning that replaces and supersedes the previous zoning   i ncluding   residential uses   among the   permitted use s  within the Redevelopment Area .  A review of New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection ( the “ NJDEP ” ) database reveals that   the  Selecto - Flash   Site   is a known contaminated site . Additionally, the Biddleman   S ite was previously subjected to   the  Industrial Site Recovery Act   ( the “ ISRA ” ) until the case was withdrawn on May 15, 2006.   However, the s oil and  groundwater contamination   at the Biddleman   S ite   appear to remain   above the applicable NJDEP standards .  Contami nation consists of petroleum hydrocarbons associated with a heating oil   underground storage tank  ( “ UST ” )   and chlorinated   volatile organic compounds ( “ VOCs ” )   associated with spills on site and an   aboveground  storage tank ( “ AST ” ) dating back to   1988 .   An ISRA   General Information Notice   ( “ GIN) was submitted in 2005.  An i nitial Receptor Evaluation ( “ RE ” )   was   due 1 year after discharge   wa s discovered or remediation initiated.  Based on the presence of chlorinated VOCs in the groundwater ,   a well search was to be completed 90 days  following the discovery of groundwater contamination .   Based on contaminated groundwater ,   a   vapor intrusion  ( “ VI ” ) investigation may be required considering   old groundwater data exceeds the   current   VI Groundwater  S creening Levels.   According to   NJDEP   Dataminer,   there   was an   obligation   to   acquire   a   Licensed Site  Remediation Professional ( “ LSRP ” ) due November 4, 2009 and a   LSRP Retention Form was to be completed 45  days after discharge or initiation of remediation.   Bas ed upon the information reviewed, it appears that an LSRP  was not retained.  As existing documentation indicates that groundwater and soil contamination still exist on this property, NJDEP  requires that a preliminary assessment and/or site investigation re port be prepared for this property and that the  site   be   fully   remediated   in   accordance   with   the   findings   and   recommendations   of   those   report s.   An  Environmental Constraints Map   is included as   Figure 5 .

Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36  and Block 7, Lot 22 -   Redevelopment Plan  3  1.3   REDEVELOPMENT   GOALS AND   OBJECTIVES  Through the adoption of this Redevelopment Plan, the Mayor,   Township   Council and Planning Board intend to  achieve certain objectives for th is   Redevelopment Area   as described below:  A.   To promote the   environmental remediation of all properties   in the   CVBDA .  B.   To provide for the coordinated and orderly development of vacant   B rownfield site s   in a manner consistent  with the   Township   Master   Plan   and the   adjacent   Harvard Press Redevelopment   Plan ,   and   State  Development and Redevelopment Plan.  C.   To promote the revitalization of vacant and underutilized   B rownfield site s   and surrounding properties  with opportunities for mixed - use development .  D.   To promote economic development opportunities that will generate private sector investment, produce  new jobs , increase tax ratables ,   and expand income levels.  E.   To promote redevelopment that is compatible with the unique characteristics of the site including its  location,   passenger   rail access, and   a growing arts and cultural community .  F.   To improve the physical con dition of the area by   encouraging   infrastructure improvements , including off -  street parking,   and providing for new development that will enhance the function of the area.  G.   To   ensure   high - quality   redevelopment   which   minimizes   nuisance   impacts   through   appropr iate  buffering/screening, noise control, creative site plan layout, and access management.  H.   To ensure compliance with all applicable NJDEP and other governmental agency requirements and  permits for   B rownfields redevelopment.  1. 4   PUBLIC UTILITIES AND INFRAS TRUCTURE  The   Central Avenue Redevelopment Area   is served by the   full   spectrum of infrastructure necessary to support  redevelopment including public water, storm sewers and sanitary sewers as well as private utility gas, electric and  telephone   service.   It   is   anticipated   that   future   utility   improvements   will   be   limited   to   con nections   and  rehabilitation of any substandard utility segments located on - site. There will also be a need for site remediation  associated with the past industrial use based upon future environmental investigation. If so, the installation,  operation and ma intenance of infrastructure for site remediation shall be factored into future redevelopment  activity planned within the designated redevelopment area. The following utility improvement(s) shall be required  as part of any   designated   redevelopment   (the “Red eveloper”) :  A.   The   Redeveloper   shall prepare a utility infrastructure plan for review and approval by the   Township  Engineer, New Jersey American Water ( “ NJAW ” )   and other agencies with jurisdiction. All drainage  imp rovements shall comply with NJAW   and NJDEP s tormwater management rules and regulations.  B.   All sanitary sewer and storm water flows shall be directed   as   determined by   NJAW   and   the   Township  Engineer.  C.   All utilities and cabling systems required to serve the redevelopment area shall be installed undergroun d  unless a particular utility component or system is required to be above ground by law. In that case, utility  installations   that   are   required   to   be   aboveground   shall   be   exempt   from   the   minimum   and   maximum  building   height   requirements. However, any   required above ground utility installations shall be screened  from view.  As the   Central Avenue Redevelopment Area   is currently serviced by public water, sewer, electric and gas utilities,  the intent of the Redevelopment Plan is to re - use these existing   service connections, and upgrade as required. All  associated permit and connection fees will be paid by the Redeveloper as required.

Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36  and Block 7, Lot 22 -   Redevelopment Plan  4  2.   DEVELOPMENT REGULATIONS  2.1   GENERAL REGULATIONS  2.1.1   Overview  Section 2, the Development Regulations, fulfills the requirement of   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A - 7a(2) to identify the land  uses and building requirements of the Redevelopment Plan.  2.1.2   Definitions  The use and understanding of terms shall be consistent with the def initions listed at 40A:12A - 3 of the Local  Redevelopment and Housing Law, 40:55D - 3 to 40:55D - 7 of the Municipal Land Use Law and   Section   25 - 4   of the  Township   Zoning   Code .  2.1.3   Word Usage  A.   Words used in the present tense include the singular number as well   as the plural.  B.   The word “lot” includes the word “plot . ”  C.   The phrase “used” includes “arranged , ” “designed , ” “intended , ” “constructed , ” “altered , ” “maintained , ”  “occupied , ” “converted,” “rented,” “leased,” or “intended to be used . ”  D.   The term “such as , ” where   used herein, shall be considered as introducing a typical or illustrative rather  than   an   entirely   exclusive   or   inclusive   designation   of   permitted   or   prohibited   uses,   activities,  establishments or structures.  E.   “Shall” and “will” are mandatory.  F.   “May” and “sho uld” are permissive.  G.   The word “person” includes an individual, corporation, partnership or any other legal entity.  H.   Either gender shall include the other.  I.   The word “includes” or “including” shall not limit the term to the specified example; but is intended   to  extend its meaning to all other instances of like kind and character.  J.   Any word or term not defined or referenced herein in Section 2 of this Redevelopment Plan shall be used  with a meaning of standard usage as defined in Webster’s New International Dict ionary of the English  Language, unabridged and latest edition .  K.   Whenever a term is used in this Section which is not defined, but which term is defined in the Municipal  Land Use Law ( N.J.S.A.   40:55D - 1 et seq.), such term is intended to have the meaning as d efined in the  Municipal Land Use Law.  L.   The term “neighborhood commercial,” where used herein, shall be considered as a commercial area,  usually located on an arterial or collector street, providing convenience goods and services for residents of  the surroun ding area.  2. 2   LAND USE REGULATIONS  Th e   Central Avenue Redevelopment Area   p ermits the following uses:  Permitted Uses:  A.   Off - street   surface parking   for the use   of   parcels located in a nother   r edevelopment   a rea   abutting this  redevelopment area   that are   developed or to be developed for   multi - family   residential use.  B.   Public open space .

In addition to other uses permitted on Lot 22 in Block 7,  this lot may be used in conjunction with or accessory to the development of Bl ock 9, Lot 36 as provided  herein.
